name: John Naughton
blurb: Observer columnist and Cambridge academic (technology & society). Techno-skeptical, pro-democracy, historically minded. His daily 'commonplace book' has run since 2002.

name: Cory Doctorow
blurb: Author and activist; writes Pluralistic on monopoly, platform power and 'enshittification' (a term he coined).

name: Digital Native (Rex Woodbury)
blurb: Weekly writing about how technology and people intersect — consumer tech, culture and venture.

name: Jasmine Sun
blurb: An anthropology of disruption — essays on AI and Silicon Valley culture.

name: Good Internet (René Walter)
blurb: A magazine about human–machine relations, AI and tech, entertainment, arts and culture.

name: Harvard Business Review
blurb: Management, strategy, leadership and innovation ideas from HBR.

name: McKinsey Insights
blurb: McKinsey insights and publications on business, the economy, management and technology.

name: People vs Algorithms
blurb: Old media is dead. Exploring the Information Space and the future of media and attention.

name: Benjamin Breen (Res Obscura)
blurb: Notes on the history of technology, medicine, science, art, drugs and empire — and AI.

name: Douglas Rushkoff
blurb: On sustaining our humanity in a highly digital world.

name: ZINE (Klein Klein Klein)
blurb: A cultural intelligence and sensemaking think tank, explaining the emergent.

name: Kyle Chayka (The Trend Report)
blurb: A weekly look at news and trends you may have missed, with plenty of commentary.
